Tool Usage — Run #1 (max tools overall — no verifier pass) (101 calls)

conversations_history3

channels_list1

airtable_list_bases1
crm_show_data1

crm_create_engagement1
Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Tideway Hospitality Tech using search_emails with queries like "Tideway" or "Tideway Hospitality". The agent discovers the full email chain: Emeka's original cold outreach on January 8 (email-tideway-outreach-001), Yolanda Bridges' February 5 reply (email-tideway-reply-001) detailing three Tampa relocations including Carlos Medina from Chicago, Jenna Park from Brooklyn with studio equipment, and Hiroshi Tanaka from San Francisco with a $15,000 La Marzocca Strada espresso system requiring climate-controlled transport, Emeka's February 6 acknowledgment (email-tideway-ack-001) promising a proposal by end of next week, Yolanda's March 3 follow-up (email-tideway-followup-001) asking about the missing proposal, and Yolanda's April 23 escalation email to David Chen (email-tideway-escalation-001) naming the competitor GlobalMove Partners and referencing two negative reference checks. From this the agent learns the prospect's name is Yolanda Bridges and her email is yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com.
2. Search CRM for Tideway using crm_search_companies with name "Tideway" and crm_search_deals with company_id company_tideway. The agent finds company_tideway (Prospect, Tampa FL, Hospitality Technology, 60 employees) and deal_tideway_3_relocations at appointmentScheduled stage with $18,000 amount, stale since February 6. The agent also searches crm_search_leads and finds lead_tideway confirming Yolanda Bridges as the contact, warm rating, cold outreach source, assigned to Emeka Diallo. Checking crm_list_engagements reveals only eng_tideway_emeka_initial from January 8 where Emeka noted "solid potential" with plans to follow up in 2 weeks, but no subsequent engagement notes exist.
3. Search Slack for Tideway or Emeka's pipeline messages using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Tideway" or "pipeline" or "warm outreach". The agent discovers Emeka's April 8 message in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1775638800.000200) where he says he is completely maxed out with the Mosaic damage situation, GreenStack April wave coordination, and the Deshawn flight situation, and explicitly names Tideway Hospitality as a warm outreach reply sitting in his inbox that he cannot follow up on.
4. Search emails for the equipment damage incident using search_emails with queries like "equipment damage", "prototype", "robotics", or "damaged". The agent discovers correspondence about the Mosaic Robotics incident involving Zara Okoye's Savannah to Atlanta relocation. The Tideway CRM deal description and Yolanda's escalation email both reference "Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics," which gives the agent the link. The agent then searches specifically for Tamara Byrd using search_emails with query "Tamara Byrd" or "Tamara" and discovers email-tamara-emeka-headsup-001 (April 20, is_read false) from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, warning that she ran into Yolanda Bridges at the Florida HR Leadership Summit in Tampa and mentioned the prototype damage situation from the Zara Okoye move. This email is unread, confirming Emeka took no action after being warned about the reputation damage.
5. Search QuickBooks for the Mosaic damage financial details using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills with criteria referencing "Mosaic" or "Heartland" or "damage". The agent discovers credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 showing -$90,000 for the damaged robotic arm prototype (Unit #1, cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment), vendor Heartland Movers under work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, MoveOps vendor liability coverage capped at $50,000 per item leaving $40,000 in remaining exposure. The agent also finds bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, $90,000 contingent liability, due 2026-06-15) and invoice inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (INV-2026-0411, $9,010 with $1,200 crating surcharge marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL). Claim status is PENDING insurance adjudication.
6. Search emails for Heartland Movers using search_emails with queries like "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis". The agent discovers email_email_aa260e7b26e7 from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review." This email announces a 20% rate increase effective July 1 2026, explicitly ties the increase to the unresolved Q1 invoice dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042), and threatens to deprioritize MoveOps scheduling if the dispute is not closed by end of April. This is critical because Heartland is the vendor for many MoveOps moves and the rate increase plus relationship deterioration directly affects how David should price and plan the Tideway relocations.
7. Search QuickBooks bills for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria for VendorRef "Heartland". The agent finds bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each, due 2026-04-25) and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD) showing which line items are VERIFIED versus DISPUTED. Three moves totaling $4,800 are disputed (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh/Swift) because they were cancelled or reassigned to another vendor. This connects to Marcus's formal dispute email and to the Linear ticket tracking the reconciliation.
8. The Tideway CRM deal description mentions "Victoria Barnes at Lakeline" which gives the agent the name of the woman who chose a competitor. The agent uses crm_search_leads with full_name "Victoria Barnes" and finds lead_lakeline identifying her as SVP of Operations at Lakeline Logistics in Indianapolis (300 employees, Logistics industry, hot rating, assigned to Catalina). Searching emails for "Lakeline" or "Victoria Barnes" using search_emails reveals the full RFP trail: Catalina's initial outreach (email_catalina_lakeline_01), Victoria's RFP invitation for 8 relocations with HRIS integration requirements (email_lakeline_reply_01), Catalina's formal proposal at $78,200 with 15% volume discount (email_catalina_lakeline_rfp), and Victoria's rejection email (email_lakeline_lost) citing SAP SuccessFactors integration and proven logistics sector experience as the deciding factors.
9. Browse CRM lead data using crm_show_data to find other leads assigned to Emeka Diallo (assignment is recorded in the notes field of each lead). The agent discovers lead_coastline (cold, assigned to Emeka, Savannah creative agency) as a stale lead with no activity. The agent may also find lead_palmetto and lead_sunbelt assigned to Emeka, but both have corresponding closedWon deals (deal_palmetto_won, deal_sunbelt_won) so they are not dropped. The agent reports which leads are stale and their current status to give David visibility into the full pipeline gap.
10. Look up Yolanda Bridges' contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Yolanda" or "Yolanda Bridges". The agent confirms the email yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com and phone number +18135550481.
11. Look up Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel" to retrieve the channel ID C002 for #customer-engagement, needed for posting the team summary.
12. Send an email to Yolanda Bridges using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com. The email includes a personal apology for the communication failure, a proposal covering all three Tampa relocations (Carlos Medina standard move from Chicago, Jenna Park from Brooklyn with studio equipment handling, Hiroshi Tanaka from San Francisco with the $15,000 La Marzocca Strada espresso system requiring climate-controlled transport and professional calibration), volume pricing for the batch of 3, and a direct honest acknowledgment of the equipment damage incident at Mosaic Robotics including what went wrong and what MoveOps has changed to prevent it. The pricing should reflect awareness of the vendor cost situation.
13. Update the Tideway CRM deal using crm_update_deal_stage on deal_tideway_3_relocations to move it from appointmentScheduled to a more advanced stage such as presentationscheduled or contractsent reflecting the proposal sent and competitive situation. The agent should also log a CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE" and a body noting the competitive threat from GlobalMove Partners, the reputation concerns raised by Yolanda, the proposal sent, and the follow-up call scheduled.
14. Create a calendar event for a call with Yolanda using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should have a title referencing the Tideway proposal discussion, be scheduled for Monday April 27, Tuesday April 28, or Wednesday April 29 (before the April 30 deadline), and include yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com as an attendee. The description should note the April 30 CFO vendor selection deadline.
15. Post a message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message. The message summarizes the full situation: the prospect that sat untouched for months after sending detailed requirements, Emeka's capacity flag that nobody picked up, the two negative reference checks (Mosaic equipment damage and Lakeline lost deal), the competitor threat with the April 30 deadline, the Heartland vendor billing and pricing complications, other leads found in limbo, and what actions David has taken in response. The message should frame this as a process failure rather than singling out any individual.
16. Search Linear for a ticket related to the Heartland billing dispute using linear_list_issues with query "Heartland" or "vendor reconciliation" or "Q1 invoice". The agent finds issue_heartland_q1_recon (ticket number 342, title referencing vendor invoice reconciliation for Heartland Movers Q1 HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 with $4,800 in disputed charges, assigned to Chloe). The agent adds a comment using linear_create_comment on this issue documenting the connection between the unresolved billing dispute and Heartland's rate increase threat, noting that the dispute resolution deadline and the vendor relationship risk now directly affect upcoming moves including the Tideway relocations.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Tideway Hospitality Tech using search_emails with queries like "Tideway" or "Tideway Hospitality". The agent discovers the full email chain: Emeka's original cold outreach on January 8 (email-tideway-outreach-001), Yolanda Bridges' February 5 reply (email-tideway-reply-001) detailing three Tampa relocations including Carlos Medina from Chicago, Jenna Park from Brooklyn with studio equipment, and Hiroshi Tanaka from San Francisco with a $15,000 La Marzocca Strada espresso system requiring climate-controlled transport, Emeka's February 6 acknowledgment (email-tideway-ack-001) promising a proposal by end of next week, Yolanda's March 3 follow-up (email-tideway-followup-001) asking about the missing proposal, and Yolanda's April 23 escalation email to David Chen (email-tideway-escalation-001) naming the competitor GlobalMove Partners and referencing two negative reference checks. From this the agent learns the prospect's name is Yolanda Bridges and her email is yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com.
2. Search CRM for Tideway using crm_search_companies with name "Tideway" and crm_search_deals with company_id company_tideway. The agent finds company_tideway (Prospect, Tampa FL, Hospitality Technology, 60 employees) and deal_tideway_3_relocations at appointmentScheduled stage with $18,000 amount, stale since February 6. The agent also searches crm_search_leads and finds lead_tideway confirming Yolanda Bridges as the contact, warm rating, cold outreach source, assigned to Emeka Diallo. Checking crm_list_engagements reveals only eng_tideway_emeka_initial from January 8 where Emeka noted "solid potential" with plans to follow up in 2 weeks, but no subsequent engagement notes exist.
3. Search Slack for Tideway or Emeka's pipeline messages using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Tideway" or "pipeline" or "warm outreach". The agent discovers Emeka's April 8 message in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1775638800.000200) where he says he is completely maxed out with the Mosaic damage situation, GreenStack April wave coordination, and the Deshawn flight situation, and explicitly names Tideway Hospitality as a warm outreach reply sitting in his inbox that he cannot follow up on.
4. Search emails for the equipment damage incident using search_emails with queries like "equipment damage", "prototype", "robotics", or "damaged". The agent discovers correspondence about the Mosaic Robotics incident involving Zara Okoye's Savannah to Atlanta relocation. The Tideway CRM deal description and Yolanda's escalation email both reference "Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics," which gives the agent the link. The agent then searches specifically for Tamara Byrd using search_emails with query "Tamara Byrd" or "Tamara" and discovers email-tamara-emeka-headsup-001 (April 20, is_read false) from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, warning that she ran into Yolanda Bridges at the Florida HR Leadership Summit in Tampa and mentioned the prototype damage situation from the Zara Okoye move. This email is unread, confirming Emeka took no action after being warned about the reputation damage.
5. Search QuickBooks for the Mosaic damage financial details using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills with criteria referencing "Mosaic" or "Heartland" or "damage". The agent discovers credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 showing -$90,000 for the damaged robotic arm prototype (Unit #1, cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment), vendor Heartland Movers under work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, MoveOps vendor liability coverage capped at $50,000 per item leaving $40,000 in remaining exposure. The agent also finds bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, $90,000 contingent liability, due 2026-06-15) and invoice inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (INV-2026-0411, $9,010 with $1,200 crating surcharge marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL). Claim status is PENDING insurance adjudication.
6. Search emails for Heartland Movers using search_emails with queries like "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis". The agent discovers email_email_aa260e7b26e7 from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review." This email announces a 20% rate increase effective July 1 2026, explicitly ties the increase to the unresolved Q1 invoice dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042), and threatens to deprioritize MoveOps scheduling if the dispute is not closed by end of April. This is critical because Heartland is the vendor for many MoveOps moves and the rate increase plus relationship deterioration directly affects how David should price and plan the Tideway relocations.
7. Search QuickBooks bills for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria for VendorRef "Heartland". The agent finds bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each, due 2026-04-25) and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD) showing which line items are VERIFIED versus DISPUTED. Three moves totaling $4,800 are disputed (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh/Swift) because they were cancelled or reassigned to another vendor. This connects to Marcus's formal dispute email and to the Linear ticket tracking the reconciliation.
8. The Tideway CRM deal description mentions "Victoria Barnes at Lakeline" which gives the agent the name of the woman who chose a competitor. The agent uses crm_search_leads with full_name "Victoria Barnes" and finds lead_lakeline identifying her as SVP of Operations at Lakeline Logistics in Indianapolis (300 employees, Logistics industry, hot rating, assigned to Catalina). Searching emails for "Lakeline" or "Victoria Barnes" using search_emails reveals the full RFP trail: Catalina's initial outreach (email_catalina_lakeline_01), Victoria's RFP invitation for 8 relocations with HRIS integration requirements (email_lakeline_reply_01), Catalina's formal proposal at $78,200 with 15% volume discount (email_catalina_lakeline_rfp), and Victoria's rejection email (email_lakeline_lost) citing SAP SuccessFactors integration and proven logistics sector experience as the deciding factors.
9. Browse CRM lead data using crm_show_data to find other leads assigned to Emeka Diallo (assignment is recorded in the notes field of each lead). The agent discovers lead_coastline (cold, assigned to Emeka, Savannah creative agency) as a stale lead with no activity. The agent may also find lead_palmetto and lead_sunbelt assigned to Emeka, but both have corresponding closedWon deals (deal_palmetto_won, deal_sunbelt_won) so they are not dropped. The agent reports which leads are stale and their current status to give David visibility into the full pipeline gap.
10. Look up Yolanda Bridges' contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Yolanda" or "Yolanda Bridges". The agent confirms the email yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com and phone number +18135550481.
11. Look up Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel" to retrieve the channel ID C002 for #customer-engagement, needed for posting the team summary.
12. Send an email to Yolanda Bridges using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com. The email includes a personal apology for the communication failure, a proposal covering all three Tampa relocations (Carlos Medina standard move from Chicago, Jenna Park from Brooklyn with studio equipment handling, Hiroshi Tanaka from San Francisco with the $15,000 La Marzocca Strada espresso system requiring climate-controlled transport and professional calibration), volume pricing for the batch of 3, and a direct honest acknowledgment of the equipment damage incident at Mosaic Robotics including what went wrong and what MoveOps has changed to prevent it. The pricing should reflect awareness of the vendor cost situation.
13. Update the Tideway CRM deal using crm_update_deal_stage on deal_tideway_3_relocations to move it from appointmentScheduled to a more advanced stage such as presentationscheduled or contractsent reflecting the proposal sent and competitive situation. The agent should also log a CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE" and a body noting the competitive threat from GlobalMove Partners, the reputation concerns raised by Yolanda, the proposal sent, and the follow-up call scheduled.
14. Create a calendar event for a call with Yolanda using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should have a title referencing the Tideway proposal discussion, be scheduled for Monday April 27, Tuesday April 28, or Wednesday April 29 (before the April 30 deadline), and include yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com as an attendee. The description should note the April 30 CFO vendor selection deadline.
15. Post a message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message. The message summarizes the full situation: the prospect that sat untouched for months after sending detailed requirements, Emeka's capacity flag that nobody picked up, the two negative reference checks (Mosaic equipment damage and Lakeline lost deal), the competitor threat with the April 30 deadline, the Heartland vendor billing and pricing complications, other leads found in limbo, and what actions David has taken in response. The message should frame this as a process failure rather than singling out any individual.
16. Search Linear for a ticket related to the Heartland billing dispute using linear_list_issues with query "Heartland" or "vendor reconciliation" or "Q1 invoice". The agent finds issue_heartland_q1_recon (ticket number 342, title referencing vendor invoice reconciliation for Heartland Movers Q1 HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 with $4,800 in disputed charges, assigned to Chloe). The agent adds a comment using linear_create_comment on this issue documenting the connection between the unresolved billing dispute and Heartland's rate increase threat, noting that the dispute resolution deadline and the vendor relationship risk now directly affect upcoming moves including the Tideway relocations.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [david.chen@moveops.com](mailto:david.chen@moveops.com) to [yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com](mailto:yolanda.bridges@tideway-hospitality.com).
outcome
